pattern: Because both players always take the two smallest remaining values in the same relative order every round, replaying the whole turn-taking process is equivalent to sorting once and then swapping every adjacent pair.
hints: The two values removed in any round are always the two smallest values still left, regardless of who is doing the removing.
Once you know it's always the two smallest remaining values, sort the whole array up front instead of simulating round by round.
After sorting, walk the array two values at a time and record the second of each pair before the first, since the second-removed value is always appended before the first-removed one.

edge_inputs.description: a jumbled array with distinct values across two rounds
the smallest possible size beyond a single pair, still jumbled
the smallest allowed length with both values tied
two tied pairs, checking that equal values still pair up correctly across rounds
already strictly increasing, so sorting changes nothing about the order
every value identical across three rounds
values pinned to the extreme ends of the allowed range with duplicates

stress.description: random even-length arrays across the full length and value range

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def numberGame(self, nums):
        pool = list(nums)
        arr = []
        while pool:
            m1 = min(pool)
            pool.remove(m1)
            m2 = min(pool)
            pool.remove(m2)
            arr.append(m2)
            arr.append(m1)
        return arr
